arts.ttl: Dan Ashworth alarm bell speaks volumes about Arsenal's decision to appoint him
arts.sum: Dan Ashworth’s brief Manchester United stint as sporting director has ended prematurely, and with Arsenal mooted as his next potential venture, the Gunners have been warned about making the call

Matchday six of the Champions League sees Monaco head to Emirates Stadium on Wednesday (8pm UK) for a crunch game as both teams aim to take a huge step towards reaching the last 16. Only Barcelona and Bayern Munich (both 7) have won more home games in the Champions League since the start of last season than us (6), while our six clean sheets are the joint-most in that time along with Inter Milan.

arts.ttl: Jose Mourinho tells Arsenal transfer target to join Real Madrid instead
arts.sum: Arsenal were widely linked to signing Nico Williams during the summer transfer but ultimately signed Raheem Sterling on loan and Jose Mourinho has advised the winger to join Real Madrid instead

arts.ttl: Arteta hints Arsenal are open to January signings
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has suggested Arsenal will explore the ‘opportunity to strengthen the team’ in the January transfer window. Arsenal have had a mixed start to the season and currently sit third in the Premier League table. A draw at Fulham at the weekend leaves the Gunners six points behind leaders Liverpool, having played a game […] The post Arteta hints Arsenal are open to January signings first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Mesut Ozil tells former team-mate that Arsenal move was 'serious mistake'
arts.sum: Mesut Ozil joined Arsenal from Real Madrid for £42.5million in 2013, but his former team-mate Sami Khedira has revealed he now views the transfer as a "serious mistake"

arts.ttl: ‘Diversity is our weapon’: inside Monaco’s eclectic football approach
arts.sum: Arsenal’s Wednesday opponents reveal how an integrated policy of buy young, pay well, sell high works for them and their sole sister club, Cercle Brugge Monaco’s training ground, carved into a hillside above the principality, overlooks the Côte d’Azur. It is the sort of glamorous setting expected for a football team in one of the world’s richest corners. Fifteen nationalities file out of the changing rooms and on to the pitch to be coached by an Austrian, driving home the club’s eclectic nature. They are the senior partner in a multi‑club operation that involves one other team: Cercle Brugge, who were bought in 2017. Whereas others stockpile clubs, two is the height of the ambition here, the thinking being that it provides agility but ensures focus is not diverted. Clubs live and die by recruitment and investing in another team allows more access to talent and increased scope to develop it. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arsenal's expected XI vs Monaco as Mikel Arteta recalls forgotten man amid injury crisis
arts.sum: Arsenal are gunning to make it back-to-back victories in the Champions League and looking to respond to Sunday's 1-1 draw away to Fulham which dented their domestic title hopes

arts.ttl: Tierney could make Arsenal return - gossip
arts.sum: Scotland defender Kieran Tierney could make his return for Arsenal, while Celtic's Alistair Johnston gives an update on his race to be fit for the League Cup final.

arts.ttl: 'Proud of him': Martin Odegaard says Arsenal have a truly 'unbelievable' youngster who'll get better and better
arts.sum: Arsenal are a club very much on the up, having gone from an upper-mid table Premier League club to a regular title challenger. Mikel Arteta...

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta drops major Arsenal transfer hint amid growing injury crisis
arts.sum: Arsenal are suffering a defensive injury crisis at the moment and manager Mikel Arteta is looking to strengthen his squad when the January transfer window opens next month
